methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $150M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Distalmotion is a Series G robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $150M+ to date. The comparable universe in Robotics & Manufacturing includes one public company (277810.KQ) and eight private peers, with visible companies including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables at similar valuation tiers to support a reliable peer-median valuation.

Given data constraints, stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach. Using the $150M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.25x step-up multiplier typical of Series G advancement yields an implied valuation of $188M. This method assumes forward-looking investor appetite at the next fundraising event and reflects historical stage-to-stage capital deployment patterns in late-stage robotics.

The triangulated range is $159M to $216M, with a median of $188M. This narrow one-method analysis carries material uncertainty. Key risks include the adequacy of the raised capital proxy as a current valuation anchor, the applicability of standard stage-progression multiples to frontier robotics, and the absence of market-tested peer multiples. A more robust valuation would require either identifying additional narrowly-matched private comparables, access to recent transaction data in the sector, or explicit revenue/unit economics from Distalmotion itself.
